audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for magi, Herod's fear, star imagery, Bethlehem/Micah citation, worship/prostration, Egypt flight, massacre lament, Hosea citation, Rachel weeping, Nazarene motif, and dream guidance.

verses.restored: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea in the days of Herod the king, look, magi from the east arrived in Jerusalem,
saying, Where is the one born king of the Judeans? For we saw his star at its rising and came to bow/prostrate before him.
When Herod the king heard this, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him.
Gathering all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he inquired from them where the Messiah was to be born.
They said to him, In Bethlehem of Judea, for so it has been written through the prophet:
And you, Bethlehem, land of Judah, are by no means least among the rulers of Judah; for from you will come a ruler who will shepherd my people Israel.
Then Herod secretly called the magi and learned carefully from them the time when the star appeared.
He sent them to Bethlehem and said, Go, search carefully for the child; when you find him, report to me, so that I too may come and bow/prostrate before him.
After hearing the king, they went; and look, the star they had seen at its rising went before them until it came and stood over where the child was.
When they saw the star, they rejoiced with exceedingly great joy.
Entering the house, they saw the child with Mary his mother. They fell down and bowed/prostrated before him, opened their treasures, and offered him gifts: gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
Being warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they withdrew to their own country by another way.

After they withdrew, look, a messenger of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph, saying, Rise, take the child and his mother, flee into Egypt, and remain there until I tell you, for Herod is about to seek the child to destroy him.
He rose, took the child and his mother by night, and withdrew into Egypt.
He remained there until the death of Herod, so that what was spoken by the Lord through the prophet might be fulfilled: Out of Egypt I called my son.

Then Herod, seeing that he had been mocked by the magi, was exceedingly enraged, and he sent and killed all the boys in Bethlehem and in all its borders from two years old and under, according to the time he had carefully learned from the magi.
Then what was spoken through Jeremiah the prophet was fulfilled, saying:
A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and great mourning: Rachel weeping for her children, and she would not be comforted, because they are no more.
After Herod died, look, a messenger of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt,
saying, Rise, take the child and his mother, and go into the land of Israel, for those seeking the child's life have died.
He rose, took the child and his mother, and entered the land of Israel.
But hearing that Archelaus ruled Judea in place of his father Herod, he feared to go there. Being warned in a dream, he withdrew into the regions of Galilee.
He came and dwelled in a city called Nazareth, so that what was spoken through the prophets might be fulfilled: He will be called a Nazarene.
